title: Coordinates, Scalars, Vector2, Angles, and Units · Spiral Review
summary: A delayed playtest review reconstructs coordinates, scalars, vector2, angles, and units from revision, seed, input frames, simulation snapshots, draw records, audio cues, accessibility settings, profiles, package artifacts, and release evidence. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for unit-labeled 2D geometry model after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Declare screen origin, axes, handedness, logical viewport, centers, bounds, and world-to-screen conversion.
Use Vector2 addition, subtraction, scaling, magnitude, squared distance, dot, cross, copy, and mutation with explicit meaning.
Derive facing vectors from a named zero-angle and degree convention and test clockwise screen appearance.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Coordinate Convention · changed case · Review Coordinate Convention
steps.instruction:
review case — A review Asteroids team handles case 1604 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A review Asteroids team handles case 1379 during a low-vision laptop playtest at 125 percent scaling. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then inject a 400 millisecond frame stall and prove the update budget prevents a spiral of death. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Multiplying or dividing by delta time is optional style.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas. Artifact under review: unit-labeled 2D geometry model.

options.text: For separate evidence from confidence, run one convenient high-frame-rate happy path, omit revision, seed, input and time trace, units, seams, device changes, entity reconciliation, multichannel cues, accessibility settings, failures, recovery, and desktop transfer, then accept a playable animation as Asteroids proof. This does not satisfy Motion Units for unit-labeled 2D geometry model.
The record names the assumption, applies Motion Units, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Motion Units record says unit-labeled 2D geometry model looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the separate evidence from confidence evidence boundary for Motion Units.

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Motion Units
steps.instruction:
Write the review evidence note for Motion Units: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A review Asteroids team handles case 2998 during a keyboard-only community arcade night. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile screen coordinates, Vector2 magnitude and direction, angle convention, and explicit motion units; test empty, zero-vector, seam, low-frame-rate, focus-loss, device-loss, missing-asset, corrupted-save, and rejected cases; then replace an audio-only collision cue with synchronized text, shape, and optional sound evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Motion Units.
steps.why: Retrieving Motion Units through the concrete unit-labeled 2D geometry model case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Record what required effort · Review Motion Units, keep Motion Units connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Label position, velocity, acceleration, angle, angular velocity, seconds, and frame counts and reject unit-invalid formulas.

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates.
